title: Voice Pipeline
subtitle: Real-time voice interaction status and configuration.

freshness.paused: Auto-refresh paused (capture stopped) — static snapshot; use refresh to update
freshness.pollStale: Live updates interrupted — showing last known data while retrying
freshness.fetchFailed: Last refresh failed — showing last known data (may be stale)

notConfigured: Voice pipeline is not configured. Enable it in your Sovyx config to see status here.

degraded.title: Voice status temporarily unavailable
degraded.detail: The /api/voice/status endpoint has returned errors on {{count}} consecutive polls. Polling has slowed to 10 s; the page will recover automatically when the endpoint responds normally.

linuxMicGain.title: Microphone input gain (Linux)
linuxMicGain.noIssues: ALSA mixer gain is within a safe range — no saturation detected.
linuxMicGain.checkUnavailableBody: The ALSA mixer diagnostic failed to load. This is NOT a clean bill of health — microphone gain was not verified. Retry, or check the daemon logs.
linuxMicGain.alertTitle: Pre-ADC gain is saturating the microphone
linuxMicGain.alertBody: One or more ALSA capture controls are pinned near maximum. Every peak of intelligible speech will clip the ADC and voice detection will fail. Reset the mixer to restore a safe capture level.
linuxMicGain.resetSuccess: Reset {{count}} mixer control(s). Microphone should now capture cleanly.
linuxMicGain.amixerMissingBody: The `amixer` command is required to diagnose and reset microphone gain. Install the alsa-utils package from your distribution's package manager.
linuxMicGain.persistHintBody: ALSA resets its mixer to the system defaults on every boot. To keep the safe capture level across reboots, save the current state with alsactl store (requires sudo on most distros):

signalState.no_device_hint: Capture isn't running or no audio frames have arrived — check the input device.
signalState.live_silent_hint: The mic is delivering audio but it's silent. Check it isn't muted or held by another app.

perMindWakeWord.title: Per-Mind Wake Word
perMindWakeWord.empty: No minds yet — onboard a mind to manage its wake word here.

forget.title: Forget this mind
forget.subtitle: Right-to-erasure (GDPR Art. 17 / LGPD Art. 18 VI). Wipes all data; preserves config.
forget.warningBody: Every concept, episode, conversation, embedding and consent-ledger entry for this mind will be deleted. Mind configuration is preserved so you can re-onboard. Use preview mode first to see counts.
forget.confirmLabel: Type the mind id ({{mindId}}) to confirm
forget.dryRunLabel: Preview only (do not delete — show counts)

retention.title: Retention prune
retention.subtitle: Time-based scheduled-policy prune. Removes only AGED records per the configured horizons.
retention.horizonsHint: Server-computed from EngineConfig.tuning.retention.* + per-mind overrides. Zero means the surface is skipped.

verify.not_running: Voice was enabled but the pipeline isn't running yet. Open Voice settings and try again.
verify.auth_failure: Your session expired while enabling voice. Sign in again to finish setup.
verify.endpoint_missing: Voice routes aren't available on this Sovyx daemon. Upgrade Sovyx or contact your operator.
verify.transient_failure: Couldn't reach the voice service to verify setup. Check your network and retry.

wizard.title: Voice Setup Wizard
wizard.openHint: Walk through 4 steps to pick + test your microphone.
wizard.openHintOptional: Walk through 4 steps to pick + test your microphone (optional).
wizard.testedProceedHint: Microphone tested — proceed to enable voice.

devices.empty: No input devices found. Plug in a microphone and try again.
devices.intro: Pick the microphone you want Sovyx to use for voice input.

record.intro: We'll record 3 seconds of audio to check signal quality and warn about issues.

diagnosis.low_signal: Signal too low — speak louder or move closer to the mic.
diagnosis.clipping: Signal is clipping — reduce input gain.
diagnosis.no_audio: No audio captured — verify the device is unmuted.
diagnosis.noisy: High background noise detected — consider quieter environment.
diagnosis.device_error: Recording failed at the device — check OS-level permissions.

save.intro: Save this microphone as the Sovyx voice input device.

done.message: All set! Your microphone is configured.

error.missing_deps: Missing voice dependencies: {{deps}}. Install with: pip install 'sovyx[voice]'.
error.capture_silence: Mic captured silence — check that your input device isn't muted.
error.capture_device_contended: Mic device is in use by another application. Close it and try again.

modal.title: Train wake word
modal.subtitle: Synthesise samples and train an ONNX model for {{wake_word}}.
modal.targetSamplesHint: 200 is the conservative minimum (~30-60 min). Higher = slower, more accurate.
modal.negativesDirHint: Filesystem path with .wav files of non-wake-word audio (your speech, ambient noise, common-voice samples).

panel.title: Wake-word Training
panel.empty: No active training. Click "Train this wake word" on a mind to start.
panel.completeHint: The trained model is now in the pretrained pool. Restart the daemon (or wait for hot-reload) to use it.

models.title: Model Matrix
models.subtitle: Available models per hardware tier.
models.legend: ✦ marks your detected tier. Other columns show the reference selection each tier would use — not models installed on your machine. Muted entries tagged "roadmap" are planned models with no runtime support yet.

health.title: Capture Health
health.subtitle: Per-endpoint audio capture diagnosis, pinned overrides, and re-probe controls.
health.empty: No validated capture endpoints yet. Run the voice setup wizard to discover one.
health.warmUnavailable: Warm re-probe requires the voice pipeline to be running.

combo.sectionHint: One row per validated audio endpoint. The stored combo reopens on boot without re-running the cascade.

overrides.sectionHint: User-pinned combos that survive --reset and short-circuit the cascade.

mixerKb.sectionHint: Shipped and user-contributed mixer profiles used by L2.5 to heal factory-bad captures. Count shows shipped / user.
mixerKb.validateHint: Paste a contributor's YAML. The backend runs the same loader the daemon uses at boot and reports pydantic issues inline.

actions.confirmForget: Forget this endpoint? The next boot will re-run the cascade.

quarantine.sectionHint: §4.4.7: capture endpoints whose kernel-side audio client is in an invalidated state. Sovyx stops probing them until a physical replug or a periodic recheck recovers the device.

remediation.title: How to recover
remediation.replug: Physically unplug the device for 5 seconds, then replug it. Sovyx reacts to the OS hot-plug event and clears the quarantine automatically.
remediation.reboot: If replugging does not clear the state, reboot Windows — a driver / audio-stack upgrade is required to re-arm the audio endpoint.
remediation.watchdog: Meanwhile, Sovyx fails over to the next viable capture device and re-checks the quarantined endpoint every few minutes.

apo_degraded.label: Capture-side DSP degraded
apo_degraded.remediation: An OS-level audio enhancement (Windows Voice Clarity APO, Linux PulseAudio module-echo-cancel / PipeWire filter chain, macOS Voice Isolation) is destroying the microphone signal. Disable per-device audio enhancements in your OS sound settings. Sovyx's bypass strategies could not recover the signal automatically on this hardware.

vad_frontend_dead.label: VAD frontend dead
vad_frontend_dead.remediation: Voice activity detection is not responding to live audio. Sovyx will attempt an automatic reset; if this persists, check microphone gain and the Silero ONNX runtime version. Endpoint is NOT recoverable via cold re-probe — the cure is a daemon restart or model refresh.

format_mismatch.label: Format mismatch
format_mismatch.remediation: Capture stream shape does not match VAD expectations (16 kHz mono int16). The FrameNormalizer was asked to re-engage; if it does not recover, try selecting a different default input device or replug the current one.

driver_silent.label: Driver silent
driver_silent.remediation: The audio driver is open but not delivering audio (RMS near zero on a working stream). Sovyx will attempt a cascade re-walk; if it does not recover, check whether the microphone is muted at the OS / hardware level.

capture_dead.label: Capture dead
capture_dead.remediation: The audio substrate is fully silent — zero callbacks fired or exact-zero PCM was delivered across every host API. Distinct from 'driver silent' (working stream / RMS near zero): here the substrate itself is dead. Cure: physically unplug and replug the microphone, reboot if replug does not clear, or restart the OS audio service (Linux: `systemctl restart pulseaudio` / `systemctl --user restart pipewire`; Windows: restart 'Windows Audio' via services.msc; macOS: `sudo killall coreaudiod`).

kernel_invalidated.label: Kernel-invalidated
kernel_invalidated.remediation: The audio driver is in a wedged state that no user-mode recovery can clear. Physically unplug and replug the device, or reboot the machine if the issue persists. This is typically caused by a USB resource timeout or a mid-session driver update.

unclassified.label: Unclassified
unclassified.remediation: Sovyx encountered an unexpected diagnosis that did not match any known terminal verdict. Please file an issue at https://github.com/anthropic/sovyx/issues with the recent log output (`~/.sovyx/logs/sovyx.log` last 200 lines). This is rare and indicates a Sovyx-internal taxonomy gap that Mission H3's Gate 14 STRICT (v0.53.0) prevents from shipping in practice.

platform.title: Platform Diagnostics
platform.subtitle: Cross-OS audio stack visibility — microphone permission, host audio service state, and OS-specific anomalies surfaced from a single auth-protected probe.

windowsSection.heading: Windows
windowsSection.audioServiceDegraded: Degraded — capture is structurally unavailable until both services return to RUNNING.
windowsSection.etwEmpty: No warning-or-higher audio events in the lookback window.

macosSection.heading: macOS
macosSection.bluetoothA2dpHint: {{name}} is in playback-only (A2DP) mode — its microphone is off, so Sovyx cannot hear you even though playback works. Switch the headset to a hands-free/call profile in System Settings → Bluetooth (or disconnect and reconnect it), then retry.

platform.noBranch: Per-OS branch not populated for this host.

bypassTier.heading: Bypass tier health
bypassTier.subtitle: Per-tier attempts and success rate since the daemon started. Tier 3 (WASAPI exclusive) is the durable Voice Clarity bypass; Tiers 1 and 2 are performance optimisations layered on top.
bypassTier.tier1Description: IAudioClient3 RAW stream + Communications role on Windows. Performance optimisation for VoIP-grade endpoints; off by default in v0.26.0.
bypassTier.tier2Description: Rotates the device away from MME / DirectSound / WDM-KS to WASAPI then engages exclusive mode. Activates when the cascade winner is on a non-WASAPI host API.
bypassTier.tier3Description: Exclusive-mode capture. Bypasses every per-endpoint capture APO including Microsoft Voice Clarity. The durable Voice Clarity fix per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern #21.

calibration.title: Voice calibration
calibration.subtitle: Sovyx will tune voice for your specific hardware. Takes 8-12 minutes; please leave the microphone connected.

status.fallback: Switching to the simple setup wizard

hypothesis.H1: Audio enhancement was disrupting your microphone
hypothesis.H4: An audio filter was breaking microphone capture
hypothesis.H6: Audio diagnostic self-test could not complete
hypothesis.H9: No microphone detected on this system
hypothesis.H10: Microphone volume was set too low at the system level

platformUnsupported.title: Auto-fix calibration is Linux-only on this host.
platformUnsupported.body: The 8-12 minute hardware tune-up uses Linux-specific audio diagnostic tools that aren't available on your platform. Use the simple setup wizard below to test your microphone and speakers — it works on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

done.title: Voice is calibrated for your hardware.

rolled_back.title: Auto-rollback restored the previous state.
rolled_back.description: One of the calibration changes failed mid-way. The applier reverted every change it had already made, so your voice configuration is back to what it was before the run started. Inspect the error below + try again or use simple setup.

fallback.title: Calibration could not complete on this hardware.
fallback.subtitle: Reason: {{reason}}. Use the simple setup wizard instead.

fast_path.title: Fast-path calibration
fast_path.subtitle: Your hardware was previously calibrated. Replaying the cached profile takes about 5 seconds.

slow_path.title: Full audio tune-up
slow_path.subtitle: Running a detailed 8-12 minute audio test to detect any issues with your microphone or speakers. Please keep your microphone connected and stay nearby — we'll ask you to speak a few short phrases.

review.title: Calibration applied
review.decision_explanation: Sovyx detected your hardware setup and applied the matching audio fix automatically. Click confirm to continue. If voice still doesn't work as expected, you can re-run the calibration any time from Settings → Voice.
review.verification_failed: Calibration finished but the voice pipeline isn't running yet. Open Settings → Voice → Recalibrate to retry, or enable manually from the Voice page.
review.auth_failed: Your session expired during calibration. Sign in again to confirm voice setup.
review.endpoint_missing: Voice routes aren't available on this Sovyx daemon. Upgrade Sovyx or contact your operator.
review.transient_failure: Couldn't reach the voice service to verify setup ({{lastError}}). Check your network and retry.

hardwareDetection.audioWarning: No audio devices detected. Voice pipeline requires a microphone and speaker.

title.cooldown: Download blocked — recent failure cooldown active
title.allMirrorsExhausted: All mirror sources are currently unreachable
title.checksumMismatch: Download rejected — file integrity check failed

hint.cooldown: We wait before retrying to avoid hammering a source that was just failing. You can force-retry below or wait for the countdown.
hint.allMirrorsExhausted: Primary + every fallback mirror failed. Check your internet connection; if persistent, the upstream release may be offline.
hint.checksumMismatch: The mirror served a file whose contents don't match the pinned SHA-256. The mirror is probably drift-ing — we'll try another source on retry.
hint.network: Transient network glitch. Retry in a moment.

micTest.ready: Ready — speak to see your levels

qualityPanel.errorBody: The endpoint returns 503 until the engine registry is ready. Enable voice (or wait a few seconds) and the panel populates automatically on the next 5-second poll.

snr.title: Capture SNR distribution
snr.noSamples: No samples in the rolling 10-second window.

verdictDescription.excellent: Studio-grade capture. Moonshine + Silero operate at calibrated rates.
verdictDescription.good: Typical office desk mic with HVAC running. <1% STT degradation.
verdictDescription.degraded: Loud open-plan office, fan close to mic. STT substitution rate climbs ~3-5× per dB lost. Move the mic 30 cm closer or enable in-process noise suppression.
verdictDescription.poor: Mic in a noisy environment (cafe, vehicle). STT becomes unreliable; VAD onset latency climbs. Move the mic, enable noise suppression, or relocate the speaker.
verdictDescription.noSignal: No SNR samples in the rolling 10-second window. Typical right after boot or during a long silence run. The panel updates automatically once speech frames flow.
verdictDescription.noDevice: No audio is reaching the capture pipeline — the mic isn't running, is unplugged, or delivered no frames. This is not a warm-up; check the input device.
verdictDescription.liveSilent: The mic is delivering audio but it sits at the noise floor — no sound is reaching it. Check it isn't muted, captured by another app, or destroyed by an audio effect (APO).

mos.title: Voice quality MOS
mos.snrProxyDetail: SNR-proxy estimate (1 + SNR/7), derived from p50 = {{snr}} dB
mos.scaleHint: MOS scale: 1.0 (poor) — 4.5 (excellent). Skype/Zoom acceptable threshold is ≥ 3.5; below 3.0 is "poor" per ITU-T P.800.
mos.proxyDisclaimerBody: Sovyx ships without the optional ``dnsmos`` extras because the package is large + Windows-build-flaky. Until you install them, the MOS reading above is an SNR-derived estimate, NOT a real DNSMOS DNN inference. To enable true DNSMOS:
mos.proxyDisclaimerStep2: Restart the daemon and enable the DNSMOS engine (set ``voice_quality_metrics_enabled=true`` + ``voice_quality_engine=dnsmos``). Until live scoring runs the panel shows "installed but inactive".
mos.proxyDisclaimerStep3: The DNSMOS T4.23-T4.30 wire-up sequence (heartbeat p50/p95, alert thresholds 3.5 / 3.0, bucketing, drift) is operator-gated to keep the SNR-proxy path simple for users who don't care about DNN-based MOS.
mos.dnsmosLiveBadge: DNSMOS active — reading is live DNN inference.
mos.dnsmosInactiveBody: The speechmos extras are installed, but live DNSMOS scoring isn't running yet (the quality engine is off, the model is still warming up, or there's no recent audio). The MOS above is still an SNR-derived proxy — NOT a DNN inference.

drift.title: Noise-floor drift baseline
drift.driftFormula: Drift = mean(short window, ~60 s) − mean(long window, ~5 min). Alert fires when sustained drift exceeds the configured threshold (default 10 dB) for the configured de-flap count (default 3 heartbeats).
drift.warmingUp: Warming up — long-window baseline needs ~5 minutes of samples before drift is meaningful ({{count}} collected).

agc2.title: AGC2 controller
agc2.vadSilencedNote: ``frames_vad_silenced`` counts frames where the T4.52 VAD-feedback gate vetoed an estimator update. Non-zero only when ``voice_agc2_vad_feedback_enabled = True``.
agc2.notActive: AGC2 not active — voice pipeline isn't running OR ``voice_agc2_enabled`` is False.

setupModal.title: Set up Voice
setupModal.description: Detect your hardware and enable the voice pipeline for local speech-to-text, text-to-speech, and voice activity detection.

depsPanel.title: Voice Dependencies
depsPanel.afterInstallHint: After installing, restart the Sovyx daemon and click Enable Voice again.

audioErrorPanel.title: No Audio Hardware Detected
audioErrorPanel.body: Voice requires a microphone and speakers. Connect audio hardware, then click Enable Voice again.

silencePanel.title: Microphone Delivered Only Silence
silencePanel.body: The selected microphone opened successfully but produced no audible signal on any host API. This usually means the mic is muted in Windows privacy settings, held exclusively by another app, or physically disconnected.
silencePanel.fixHint: Fix the mic and click Enable Voice again.

deviceContention.title: Microphone is held by another audio client
deviceContention.namedBody: <0>{{processHint}}</0> is currently capturing on <1>{{hostApi}}</1>. Sovyx can try a session-manager virtual device instead.
deviceContention.anonymousBody: Another audio app (usually PipeWire, PulseAudio, or a videoconf client) is capturing on <0>{{hostApi}}</0>. Sovyx can switch to a session-manager virtual device instead — pick one below to retry.
deviceContention.noAlternatives: No enumerated alternatives — try closing the app that is holding the mic (commonly a videoconference client) and retry.

ttsTest.timeoutMessage: Timed out waiting for playback to finish.

errorCodes.disabled: Voice device test is disabled by configuration.
errorCodes.pipelineActive: Voice pipeline is running — disable it to run the test.
errorCodes.ttsUnavailable: No TTS Python package installed. Run pip install sovyx[voice].
errorCodes.modelsNotDownloaded: TTS model files are not on disk. Download them to continue.
errorCodes.deviceBusy: Output device is in use by another app.
errorCodes.unsupportedSamplerate: This device rejected the synthesis sample rate.
errorCodes.unsupportedChannels: This device rejected the requested channel layout.
errorCodes.unsupportedFormat: Windows mixer format conflict — change the device format in Windows Sound settings.
errorCodes.bufferSizeInvalid: Device buffer size rejected — try a different device or restart the audio service.

heapSnapshot.title: Heap snapshot
heapSnapshot.subtitle: Top {{count}} allocators by retained bytes (tracemalloc).
heapSnapshot.notFound: Heap snapshot #{{timestamp}} not found (may have been rotated out or tracemalloc was disabled when the cohort fired).

threadSnapshot.title: Thread snapshot
threadSnapshot.subtitle: Per-thread stacks captured from sys._current_frames() ({{count}} threads).
threadSnapshot.notFound: Thread snapshot #{{timestamp}} not found (may have been rotated out).

resources.title: Engine resources
resources.subtitle: Live per-cohort registry state (Mission H4 §T3.4). Polls /api/engine/resources every 30 s.
resources.degraded: Resource snapshot endpoint unavailable. The structured-log stream remains the source of truth.

process.title: Process
process.description: OS-level psutil metrics: RSS, threads, file descriptors, CPU.

asyncio.title: asyncio
asyncio.description: Event-loop task counts + LIVE default-executor introspection. Post-MISSION-A.1: not_done_count + awaiting_count (renamed from running/pending) + all_task_names (replaces the observation-paradox current_running_task_name field).

to_thread.title: to_thread (stale-at-last-dispatch)
to_thread.description: Pool state recorded at the LAST dispatch_to_thread() call. NOT live — for the live executor state see asyncio.default_executor_state (post-MISSION-A.1.P3.b twin-name disambiguation, ADR-D16). Per-label dispatch counters track work distribution.

gc.title: GC
gc.description: Per-generation collection counts + total tracked objects.

exception_cohort.title: Exception cohort
exception_cohort.description: Post-MISSION-A.1.P2 (ADR-D14): cumulative-vs-window split. ‘window_*’ fields decay as observations age out of the rolling window (governor reads these). ‘cumulative_*’ fields are monotonic since process start (forensic-only).

fieldTooltips.process_cpu_percent: psutil delta since last call — first snapshot reports 0.0 (calibration tick). For instantaneous CPU read this field on the second tick onwards.
fieldTooltips.process_num_handles_or_fds: Windows: psutil.num_handles() (kernel handles — files, registry, GDI, threads, etc.; typical 2,000–10,000+). POSIX: psutil.num_fds() (file descriptors only; typical 30–80). Magnitude is NOT comparable across platforms.
fieldTooltips.process_memory_percent: psutil.memory_percent() — process RSS as % of HOST physical memory. On Docker/k8s with cgroup limits the host total is NOT the container limit — 5% on host can be 80% of a 2 GiB cgroup. Inspect /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max for the container limit.
fieldTooltips.process_cpu_times_s: CUMULATIVE since process start (NOT instantaneous). For instantaneous CPU-bound classification compute Δ/Δt across successive snapshots: (cpu_times_user_s_new - cpu_times_user_s_old) / (snapshot_interval_s).
fieldTooltips.process_connections_count: psutil.net_connections() — Linux non-root sees ONLY the daemon's own connections (system-wide view requires CAP_NET_ADMIN). Count lower than `lsof -i` is permission-restricted, not under-reporting.
fieldTooltips.process_open_files_count: Null may mean: (a) intentional skip during the shutdown snapshot, (b) psutil PermissionError, or (c) psutil missing. The triple semantics are disambiguated by the parallel process.open_files_status field (MISSION-A.2.P4 F-012 closure).
fieldTooltips.asyncio_not_done_count: Count of asyncio tasks where not t.done() is True. INCLUDES tasks blocked on await asyncio.sleep, I/O, or locks — this is NOT a measure of tasks executing on the loop step. Post-MISSION-A.1.P3.b rename of running_count (anti-pattern #51, ADR-D16).
fieldTooltips.asyncio_awaiting_count: not_done_count minus the snapshotter task itself. Most workload tasks live here. Sustained growth without matching completion suggests a task leak. Post-MISSION-A.1.P3.b rename of pending_count.
fieldTooltips.asyncio_all_task_names: List of names of every not-done asyncio task (cap N=64). Replaces the pre-MISSION-A.1 current_running_task_name field that always returned the snapshotter task name (observation paradox, ADR-D15).
fieldTooltips.asyncio_default_executor_state: LIVE introspection of asyncio's default ThreadPoolExecutor at snapshot time (pool_size = created threads, queue_depth = pending submissions, max_workers = ceiling). Twin of to_thread.*_at_last_dispatch — these are LIVE; those are STALE.
fieldTooltips.to_thread_at_last_dispatch: Recorded at the LAST dispatch_to_thread() call — may be up to one snapshot interval stale if no dispatch occurred recently. For LIVE values see asyncio.default_executor_state. Post-MISSION-A.1.P3.b rename (anti-pattern #51, ADR-D16).
fieldTooltips.gc_objects_count: len(gc.get_objects()) — O(heap-size) cost per snapshot. Default 60s interval is cheap; tuning perf_hotpath_interval_seconds below ~10s on a populated process can stall the event loop.
fieldTooltips.tracemalloc_is_tracing: Allocator tracing is opt-in (SOVYX_OBSERVABILITY__FEATURES__TRACEMALLOC=true). When true the process pays ~25-30% memory overhead. current_kb + peak_kb are 0 when not tracing — that's the NOT-TRACING marker, not 'zero memory used'.
fieldTooltips.exception_cohort_window: Rolling window over the last tuning.exception_cohort_window_s seconds (default 300s). Decays as observations age out of the bounded observations deque (maxlen=128). The cohort governor reads these fields for the EXCEPTION_COHORT budget verdict — large values trigger BUDGET_EXCEEDED.
fieldTooltips.exception_cohort_cumulative: MONOTONIC ACCUMULATOR since process start. Never decays; never resets. Forensic-only — do NOT use for real-time triage; use exception_cohort.window_* instead.
fieldTooltips.exception_cohort_last_observation_monoto: time.monotonic() value at the most recent record_exception_cohort() call. Opaque process-local value — useful only for relative comparison (now - this value = age of last observation).

failoverHistory.title: Failover History
failoverHistory.subtitle: Recent runtime failover ladder runs (Mission C3 §T2.9).
failoverHistory.empty: No failover ladder has run yet on this daemon process.

degradedBanner.title: Voice failover exhausted
degradedBanner.body: Every alternative capture device was unreachable. Try reconnecting your USB microphone or restarting Sovyx.

ladderExhausted.title: Voice failover exhausted
ladderExhausted.body: All {{candidates_tried}} alternative audio devices failed. Reconnect your USB microphone or restart Sovyx.

apo_degraded.title: Capture endpoint quarantined — capture-side DSP
apo_degraded.body: An OS-level audio enhancement is destroying the microphone signal. Disable per-device enhancements in your OS sound settings.

vad_frontend_dead.title: Capture endpoint quarantined — VAD frontend dead
vad_frontend_dead.body: Sovyx's voice-activity detector is unresponsive. Restart the daemon to refresh the Silero session.

format_mismatch.title: Capture endpoint quarantined — format mismatch
format_mismatch.body: The capture stream shape does not match VAD expectations. Try a different default input device or replug.

driver_silent.title: Capture endpoint quarantined — driver silent
driver_silent.body: The audio driver opened but is not delivering signal. Check OS-level mute, then replug.

capture_dead.title: Capture endpoint quarantined — substrate dead
capture_dead.body: Every host API delivered exact-zero PCM. Physically replug the microphone or restart the OS audio service.

kernel_invalidated.title: Capture endpoint quarantined — kernel-invalidated
kernel_invalidated.body: The audio driver is in a wedged state. Replug the device or reboot if the issue persists.

unclassified.title: Capture endpoint quarantined — unclassified
unclassified.body: Sovyx encountered an unexpected verdict. File an issue with the recent log output.

bypassActivated.title: Capture-chain bypass activated
bypassActivated.body: Sovyx switched the microphone capture stream out of the {{platform}} capture chain ({{family}}) to recover signal integrity.

bypassIneffective.title: Capture-chain bypass exhausted
bypassIneffective.body: Sovyx tried every {{platform}} capture-chain bypass strategy ({{family}}) but the signal is still dead. Run `sovyx doctor voice` or reconnect the microphone.

bypassFailed.title: Capture-chain bypass error
bypassFailed.body: The bypass coordinator callback raised. Inspect recent logs for the upstream error.

noProvider.title: No LLM provider configured
noProvider.body: The cognitive loop cannot generate responses. Install Ollama or set an API key.

noProviderConfigured.title: No LLM provider configured
noProviderConfigured.body: The cognitive loop cannot generate responses. Configure a provider via `sovyx llm setup`, or install Ollama for a local fallback.

ollamaUnreachable.title: Ollama daemon unreachable
ollamaUnreachable.body: Ollama is your default provider but the daemon is not responding. Start it with `ollama serve` or restart the service.

ollamaNoModels.title: Ollama has no models
ollamaNoModels.body: Ollama is running but no models are installed. Pull a model with `ollama pull llama3.1` (or any model from the Ollama library).

cloudKeyInvalid.title: LLM API key invalid
cloudKeyInvalid.body: Every configured cloud key failed validation. Rotate the keys via the dashboard, or re-run `sovyx llm setup`.

allUnhealthy.title: All LLM providers unhealthy
allUnhealthy.body: Every configured provider is currently unreachable. Check network connectivity and use the Test Connection button per provider.

partialHealth.title: LLM partial health
partialHealth.body: Some providers are degraded but routing continues. Address the unhealthy entries in provider settings.

defaultModelUnavailable.title: Default model unavailable
defaultModelUnavailable.body: The configured default model cannot be served by any available provider. Update mind.yaml or open provider settings.

languageCoerced.title: Speech recognition language fallback
languageCoerced.body: The configured language '{{requested_language}}' is unsupported by the active STT engine. Voice will transcribe in English until a multilingual engine is installed.

partial.body: Some dashboard files are missing from this installation ({{missing_count}} chunks not found). The interface may render with broken routes or missing components. Reinstall via pipx to restore the full bundle.

index_html_missing.body: The dashboard's main entry point is missing. The interface cannot render. Reinstall sovyx, or run 'npm run build' if you're developing from source.

static_dir_missing.body: The dashboard's static assets directory is entirely absent from this installation. Reinstall sovyx to restore the bundle.

legacy_index_html_no_assets.body: The dashboard entry point exists but its companion assets are missing. This is typically caused by an incomplete or stale build. Reinstall sovyx, or run 'npm run build' if developing.

rss_growth_spike.title: Process memory growing rapidly
rss_growth_spike.body: RSS grew by {{observed}} bytes within the cohort window (budget {{budget}}). This often precedes an OOM kill. Open the resources dashboard to inspect per-cohort allocators.

thread_count_spike.title: Thread count climbing rapidly
thread_count_spike.body: Threads grew by {{observed}} within the cohort window (budget {{budget}}). A failover-replay storm or unbounded executor pool is likely. Inspect the thread snapshot under diagnostics.

lock_dict_cardinality_saturated.title: Lock-dict aggregate cardinality saturated
lock_dict_cardinality_saturated.body: Aggregate LRULockDict cardinality reached {{observed}} (soft cap {{budget}}). One LRULockDict is likely missing an eviction policy. Restart the daemon if the growth continues.

onnx_session_unexpected_count.title: Unexpected ONNX session count
onnx_session_unexpected_count.body: Live ONNX InferenceSession count is {{observed}} (expected {{budget}}). Sessions are not being released — likely a leaked reference. Restart the daemon if growth continues.

exception_cohort_retention_high.title: ExceptionGroup window retention high
exception_cohort_retention_high.body: Rolling-window retained bytes from recent ExceptionGroup chains is {{observed}} (cap {{budget}}). The window decays as observations age out of the bounded deque (default exception_cohort_window_s=300s) — the breach will auto-clear when retention drops back under cap. Inspect the structured-log stream for repeated failures driving the storm.

heap_snapshot_triggered.title: Heap snapshot captured
heap_snapshot_triggered.body: A forensic heap snapshot was persisted to {{heap_snapshot_path}}. Click the action chip to view the top allocators.
